entities.name: SEED LONGEVITY
entities.description: A measure of the viability of seeds during long-term storage, crucial for conservation and crop improvement

entities.name: LONG-TERM STORAGE
entities.description: The state of keeping seeds for extended periods, during which viability is measured

entities.name: GERMPLASM CONSERVATION
entities.description: The process of preserving genetic resources for future use

entities.name: CROP IMPROVEMENT PROGRAMS
entities.description: Initiatives aimed at enhancing the genetic traits of crops

entities.name: FOOD AND NUTRITIONAL SECURITY
entities.description: The state of having reliable access to a sufficient quantity of affordable, nutritious food

entities.name: SEED DETERIORATION
entities.description: The irreversible process of loss of seed viability involving DNA and membrane damage

entities.name: CEREAL CROPS
entities.description: Grass crops grown for their edible seeds, such as rice, wheat, maize, and barley

entities.name: AGRICULTURAL PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: The measure of agricultural output per unit of input

entities.name: FOOD SECURITY
entities.description: The availability and access to food for all people at all times

entities.name: DNA INTEGRITY
entities.description: The state of DNA being undamaged and functionally intact

entities.name: MEMBRANE DAMAGE
entities.description: The degradation of cellular membranes during seed deterioration

entities.name: INACTIVATION OF STORAGE ENZYMES
entities.description: The loss of function of enzymes responsible for seed storage

entities.name: MITOCHONDRIAL DYSFUNCTION
entities.description: The impairment of mitochondrial activity during seed deterioration

entities.name: BIOTECHNOLOGICAL TOOLS
entities.description: Tools used to manipulate biological systems to improve seed storage potential

entities.name: GENETICS
entities.description: The study of genes and heredity used to identify regions regulating seed longevity

entities.name: GENOMICS
entities.description: The study of the complete set of DNA used to identify longevity traits in cereals

entities.name: RICE
entities.description: A major cereal crop studied for seed longevity

entities.name: WHEAT
entities.description: A major cereal crop studied for seed longevity

entities.name: MAIZE
entities.description: A major cereal crop studied for seed longevity

entities.name: BARLEY
entities.description: A major cereal crop studied for seed longevity

entities.name: POACEAE
entities.description: The grass family that includes cereals and millets

entities.name: MILLETS
entities.description: Small-seeded grasses in the Poaceae family

entities.name: OMICS TOOLS
entities.description: Integrated high-throughput technologies including genomics, proteomics, metabolomics, and phenomics

entities.name: PROTEOMICS
entities.description: The large-scale study of proteins as part of omics tools

entities.name: METABOLOMICS
entities.description: The study of small-molecule metabolite profiles as part of omics tools

entities.name: PHENOMICS
entities.description: The study of physical and biochemical traits as part of omics tools

entities.name: MOLECULAR DETERMINANTS
entities.description: The precise genetic and molecular factors affecting the survivability of seeds

entities.name: BIOTECHNOLOGICAL APPROACHES
entities.description: Strategies used to manipulate genes and genomic regions to develop improved cultivars
